data.title: Lendbuzz files for IPO with SEC, utilizing AI and ML for consumer credit risk, aiming to list common stock on The NASDAQ.
data.description: Boston-based financial technology company Lendbuzz Inc. has revealed its plans for an initial public offering (IPO) of its common stock. The company, which leverages artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms to enhance the evaluation of consumer credit risk and increase credit availability for vehicle purchases, filed a registration statement on Form S-1 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Lendbuzz aims to list its common stock on The NASDAQ exchange. This move signifies a significant step for Lendbuzz in reaching a broader investor base and expanding its market presence.

data.title: Inflation rising to 2.9% YoY, OPEN stock rallied 80% overnight. Fed likely to cut rates next week, uncertainty on the amount.
data.description: Inflation remains a concern for the Federal Reserve as it is not decreasing rapidly enough, with housing potentially showing signs of improvement from its recent decline. The most recent CPI report for August revealed that inflation rose to 2.9% year-over-year from 2.7% in July, while core inflation held steady at 3.1%. Monthly inflation also increased by approximately 0.4% on a seasonally adjusted basis. Despite these numbers, the Fed is expected to cut rates next week, with a 100% probability of a rate reduction, although the likelihood of a 50 basis points cut versus a 25 basis points cut stands at 7.5%. The market anticipates a rate cut in September, with the key questions now being the extent of the cut and the Fed's confidence in future actions. Certain sectors such as food and shelter are experiencing persistent inflation, which may deter the Fed from implementing aggressive rate cuts. However, the overall trend suggests that a easing monetary policy is gaining momentum. The upcoming FOMC meeting will provide further insights. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.description: An initial payment of $4.7 million was made, with the potential for an additional approximate total of $8.3 million in gross proceeds upon the full exercise of warrants.

data.title: Prenetics reports remarkable growth in second quarter 2025, with $32.3M revenue, aiming for $100M annual revenue by year-end. IM8 achieved $5.9M revenue in Aug 2025, pursuing $9M monthly goal. Company holding 228 Bitcoin, adding 1 daily. Target break-even in Q1 2026.	Read more: https://www.benzinga.com/pressreleases/25/09/g23399835/prenetics-announces-second-quarter-2025-financial-results-with-im8-on-track-to-achieve-100m-annual-reven
data.description: Prenetics Global Limited, a health sciences company, reported impressive financial results for the second quarter of 2025 with revenue of $17.7 million and a gross profit of $7.3 million. They also increased their revenue guidance range for FY2025 to $85-$100 million. IM8, their supplement brand, achieved $5.9 million in revenue in August 2025, marking a remarkable CAGR of over 3,100%. Prenetics added a total of 228 Bitcoins to their Treasury, accumulating 1 BTC daily. With a cash balance of $90 million and a clear path to profitability in Q1 2026, Prenetics aims to achieve break-even and be EBITDA positive. IM8 Health demonstrated strong growth, with monthly revenue surging from $581,000 to $5.9 million in August 2025. The brand has processed over 300,000 customer orders, delivering over 8 million servings of its products. Prenetics also divested ACT Genomics and plans to increase their Bitcoin accumulation rate, believing in Bitcoin as a store of value. Their full-year revenue guidance for 2025 is between $85-$100 million, and they expect to achieve break-even in Q1 2026. Prenetics aims to deliver significant long-term value to shareholders through their strategic vision and operational performance.

data.title: Investor rights law firm Halper Sadeh LLC investigates HBI, BFIN, PBBK, SONN for violations, breaches. Sept. 12, 2025.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, Halper Sadeh LLC, a law firm specializing in investor rights, has launched investigations into several companies regarding possible breaches of federal securities laws and fiduciary duties to shareholders. One of the companies under scrutiny is HanesBrands Inc. (NYSE: HBI), which is being investigated in relation to its sale. The firm is examining potential violations that may have occurred during this transaction.

data.description: The value of Dogecoin increased by approximately 4% to reach $0.26, even as Bloomberg's Eric Balchunas announced a further postponement for the first US Dogecoin exchange-traded fund (ETF).

data.title: FOX News presents "Charlie Kirk: An American Original" hosted by Jesse Watters on Sept 13 at 7 PM/ET.
data.description: On Saturday, September 13th at 7 PM/ET, FOX News Channel is set to air a special program titled "Charlie Kirk: An American Original" hosted by Jesse Watters. The one-hour primetime special will showcase the life and achievements of Charlie Kirk. This tribute marks a significant event, with viewers tuning in to watch the special and celebrate an American original.

data.title: Illumination and Nintendo announce new Super Mario animated film titled The Super Mario Galaxy Movie; to be released worldwide on April 3, 2026.
data.description: Illumination and Nintendo have revealed that their upcoming animated film based on Super Mario Bros. will be titled The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. The movie is set to be released globally by Universal Pictures on April 3, 2026. Illumination, headquartered in Santa Monica, CA, USA, and founded by CEO Chris Meledandri, will be partnering with Nintendo, based in Kyoto, Japan, with Shuntaro Furukawa as the President. The collaboration aims to bring the beloved video game characters to the big screen in what is anticipated to be a highly-anticipated cinematic event.

data.title: Approximately 50% of PancakeSwap's 'random' prize winners seem connected as revealed by blockchain analysis, contrary to claims.
data.description: PancakeSwap recently announced the winners of its trading competition, stating that they were chosen randomly. However, an analysis of blockchain records reveals that more than 50% of the winners have connections to a group of interlinked wallets. This discrepancy raises concerns about the fairness and transparency of the competition.
